name: Raised Donuts
recipeInstructions:
1. Scald milk and let it cool until lukewarm.
2. Add softened yeast (in lukewarm water) to the cooled milk mixture along with nutmeg or cinnamon.
3. Mix in enough flour to form a soft roll dough consistency. Cover and let rise overnight.
4. The next morning, punch down the dough and add sugar, eggs, and additional flour to form a soft roll dough. Cover and let rise until doubled in bulk.
5. Turn onto lightly floured board and roll into a sheet about 1/3 inch thick.
6. Cut with a lightly floured cutter.
7. Cook in deep, hot fat (365°F).
8. Drain on crumpled, absorbent paper. The doughnuts should rise to the top and brown almost instantly.
9. Makes approximately 48 doughnuts.
Florence Taft Eaton
Concord, MA
